plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-252 RP07.P04.M06.S03-RP07.P05.M02.S14 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-252 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP07.P04 UI tail (M06 fixture rows, M07-M10 cycles) and opens the RP07.P05 fixture/golden foundation (M00-M02 head) as eight descriptor sections over 150 planned rows, including base tenant/user/matter/document fixtures, golden/review/denied/cross-tenant/missing-context cases, audit hint cases, security trimming, AI retrieval cases, fixture manifests, golden/failure tests, Hermes fixture evidence, Claude missing-test prompts, and no-real-data checks, with Hermes evidence, Claude review packet, and CP00-253 handoff, without opening UI runtime execution, AI runtime dispatch, search/OCR/index/embedding runtime, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, email, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-252 (Risk C, 150 units, RP07.P04.M06.S03-RP07.P05.M02.S14) is a descriptor-only search-core pack that closes the RP07.P04 UI tail and opens the RP07.P05 fixture/golden foundation. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is supported and not overclaimed: manifest and command-evidence both report production_ready:false with status implemented_pending_review/implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout. Pack identity, range, upstream/next, distributions (deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title all summing to 150), and the 8 required section row-sets are mutually consistent across registry.js, service.js, validators.js, contracts/search-core-contract.json, scripts/validate-rp07-search-core-contract.mjs, and test/model.test.js. All 7 required public exports exist and resolve via index.js export * (explaining why index.js is unmodified). Descriptor-only and no-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries hold: every row sets descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false; freeze attestations zero out all runtime/dispatch/write/storage/audit flags; no writeFileSync/fetch/DB/process.env/network calls or PII/secret patterns appear in source. The Claude read-only / not-final-approver boundary is preserved (claude_is_final_approval:false, read_only:true, allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false), and the validator deep-equals contract vs registry constants to prevent drift. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic evidence-packet metadata (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, no-write receipt shape), not product runtime. No prior review evidence requires rejection (no raw-output/receipt present yet; invalid_attempts_preserved empty). Note: the listed validation commands could not be independently executed under the read-only mandate; this review verifies source/artifact consistency supporting the recorded exit_code:0 evidence. One P3 documentation-clarity observation on the harness authority-boundary wording; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout. Final enterprise-trust closure remains a human responsibility.

findings.title: authority_boundary token enterprise_trust_claim_closed:true reads as ambiguous against human_final_approval_required:true
findings.details: The harness-provided authority_boundary sets enterprise_trust_claim_closed:true (review-baseline.json line 1543; identical block in prompt.txt) while the adjacent field human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim:true (line 1542) and review-packet.md ('Human/final approval and enterprise trust claim: closed outside this receipt') indicate the claim is deferred to human approval, not satisfied by this review. Read in isolation, the boolean enterprise_trust_claim_closed:true could be misread as the enterprise trust claim already being satisfied. The pack implementation itself is unambiguous and correct: descriptor.authority_boundary uses claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, and the NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION sets claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false (asserted in validators.js and the validator script). This is a wording/clarity nit in the review harness boundary token, not a defect in the pack code, and does not weaken any gate.
findings.recommendation: For future review packets, rename or annotate the token (e.g., enterprise_trust_claim_closed_outside_this_receipt) so the deferred-to-human semantics are unambiguous and cannot be read as a satisfied enterprise-trust claim. No change required to CP00-252 implementation.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; non-blocking. Surfaced for the human final approver. Pack closeout may proceed; enterprise-trust closure remains gated on human approval outside this receipt.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-252 search core p04 closeout p05 fixture foundation descriptor pack
